evals.prompt: CI just went red on MR !84. Watch it, fix whatever broke, and re-push until the pipeline is green.
evals.expected_output: Parses the MR reference !84, detects the platform from repo context, and confirms CLI auth. Enters the monitor loop, reads MR state, and finds a failing CI run. Fetches the failure detail and classifies it (for example a lint or type error). Applies a minimal targeted change to only the offending code, commits with a ticket prefix inferred from recent git history, and pushes. Resumes monitoring the same MR without exiting, waiting for the re-triggered pipeline result.

assertions.name: red-ci-triage
assertions.description: Detects the failed CI run, fetches the failure detail, and classifies the failure category (lint / style / type) rather than guessing blindly.

assertions.name: targeted-patch-repush
assertions.description: Applies a minimal fix scoped to the failing check only, commits with a ticket prefix derived from recent commit history (not hardcoded), pushes, and re-enters the watch loop instead of stopping.

evals.prompt: A reviewer left a change request on MR !61. Keep watching it and handle their feedback so it can merge.
evals.expected_output: Parses the MR reference !61 and enters the monitor loop. Detects an unresolved discussion carrying a reviewer change request. Makes the minimal edit that satisfies the requested change, commits with a history-derived ticket prefix, and pushes. Continues looping over MR state, CI status, and outstanding discussions until the MR reaches a terminal state, rather than declaring victory after a single push.

assertions.name: reviewer-change-request
assertions.description: Identifies the unresolved reviewer discussion requesting changes and responds with a scoped code change, not an unrelated rewrite.

assertions.name: watch-until-terminal
assertions.description: Keeps looping over MR state, CI, and discussions after pushing the fix; only stops on merged / closed / user-stop / safety limit.

evals.prompt: MR !77 has a merge conflict. Rebase it, resolve the conflict, and confirm once it actually merges.
evals.expected_output: Parses the MR reference !77 and enters the monitor loop. Detects that the branch needs a rebase and has a merge conflict. Rebases, resolves the conflicting hunks, commits with a ticket prefix inferred from recent git history, and pushes. Continues monitoring until the platform reports the MR as merged, then prints a summary and exits the loop without sleeping or re-entering another watch cycle.

assertions.name: conflict-rebase-resolve
assertions.description: Detects the rebase-needed / conflict condition, rebases, and resolves the conflicting changes before pushing.

assertions.name: merge-confirmed-clean-exit
assertions.description: Once the MR is reported merged, emits a summary and exits immediately; does not sleep or start a fresh monitor loop after the terminal state.
